title-group.article-title: Bell and CHSH Correlations from Quaternionic Spin Geometry: A Direct Derivation on S^3

abstract.p: This paper gives a direct derivation of Bell and Clauser-Horne-Shimony-Holt (CHSH) correlations using the fixed quaternion-SU(2) convention established in qqc-001-foundations. The analyzer settings are represented by pure unit quaternions u_a and u_b in the imaginary subspace of H, so that their product decomposes as u_a u_b = -a·b + u_{a×b}. In the singlet sector, that scalar-vector decomposition yields the standard joint probabilities P(s,t|a,b) = 1/4(1 - st a·b), the correlation law E(a,b) = -a·b = Sc(u_a u_b), and the CHSH operator bound |S| <= 2 sqrt(2). The mathematical predictions are the standard quantum-mechanical ones; the project-specific contribution is the geometric organization.

license.license-p: This draft is distributed under the terms of the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License.

sec.title: 1. Introduction
sec.p: This paper derives the singlet Bell correlation law and the CHSH bound in the fixed quaternion-SU(2) convention of the RQM paper series. The mathematical predictions are the standard quantum-mechanical ones; the contribution is the geometric organization.

sec.title: 2. Quaternionic analyzer geometry
sec.p: For pure analyzer quaternions u_a and u_b, quaternion multiplication gives u_a u_b = - a dot b + u_(a cross b). The scalar part therefore equals minus the analyzer dot product.

sec.title: 3. Singlet-sector probabilities and Bell correlation
sec.p: Using the singlet density matrix and the projector family Pi_s(a) = 1/2(I + s a dot sigma), the joint probabilities are P(s,t|a,b) = 1/4(1 - s t a dot b). Summing over outcomes gives E(a,b) = - a dot b = Sc(u_a u_b).

sec.title: 4. CHSH bound and optimal configuration
sec.p: The CHSH operator obeys the standard Tsirelson bound |S| <= 2 sqrt(2). In the quaternionic language, the relevant commutators are cross-product statements induced by the pure-quaternion commutator.

sec.title: 5. Standard mathematics versus RQM framing
sec.p: The probabilities and bounds proved here are standard. The project-specific contribution is the use of quaternionic analyzer geometry as a compact way to organize them, together with the interpretive statement that Bell excludes local hidden-variable reductions, not quaternionic spin geometry itself.

ref.mixed-citation: Bell, J. S. On the Einstein Podolsky Rosen paradox. Physics Physique Fizika. 1964; 1(3):195-200.
Clauser, John F.; Horne, Michael A.; Shimony, Abner; Holt, Richard A. Proposed experiment to test local hidden-variable theories. Physical Review Letters. 1969; 23(15):880-884.
Tsirelson, B. S. Quantum generalizations of Bell's inequality. Letters in Mathematical Physics. 1980; 4(2):93-100.
